human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for kitchen-production-specific coordination behavior, source quality, pricing and refund boundaries, food-safety and fraud escalation, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to kitchen production coordination, owner routing, blocker tracking, KDS and expo handoffs, and closure confirmation rather than generic restaurant execution.

Authority sources cover FDA Food Code, FDA menu-labeling and allergen guidance, New York reservation and delivery statutes, NYC DCWP delivery-app requirements, FTC restaurant delivery enforcement, and PCI DSS.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope work, missing prerequisites, duplicate ownership, booking or payment mismatches, refund-boundary rejection, guest-safety and fraud escalation, execution-boundary rejection, high-volume rush handling, low-confidence handling, conflicting-policy escalation, tenant adaptation, and closure confirmation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/food-service-and-restaurants/kitchen-production-coordination-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent guest-service, pricing, fraud, safety, and operations lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated kitchen production coordination specialist for dependency intake, handoff packaging, blocker escalation, ticket-aware routing, and closure confirmation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
